heading: Exemption for United States and possessions
label: 4293 Exemption for United States and possessions
text: The Secretary of the Treasury may authorize exemption from the taxes imposed by section 4041, section 4051, chapter 32 (other than the taxes imposed by sections 4064 and 4121) and subchapter B of chapter 33, as to any particular article, or service or class of articles or services, to be purchased for the exclusive use of the United States, if he determines that the imposition of such taxes with respect to such articles or services, or class of articles or services will cause substantial burden or expense which can be avoided by granting tax exemption and that full benefit of such exemption, if granted, will accrue to the United States.
notes: Amendments2014— struck out “subchapter A of chapter 31,” after “imposed by”.Pub. L. 113–2951990— inserted “subchapter A of chapter 31,” before “section 4041”.Pub. L. 101–5081988— inserted reference to .Pub. L. 100–647section 4051 of this title1978— substituted “taxes imposed by sections 4064 and 4121” for “tax imposed by section 4121”.Pub. L. 95–618substituted “section 4041, chapter 32” for “chapters 31 and 32”.Pub. L. 95–502inserted “(other than the tax imposed by section 4121)” after “chapters 31 and 32”.Pub. L. 95–2271976— substituted “Secretary of the Treasury” for “Secretary” after “The”.Pub. L. 94–4551970— substituted “subchapter B” for “subchapters B and C”.Pub. L. 91–258
